title: Today's Markets Brief
summary: A look at the latest finance news

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Markets Brief. I've been thinking about trust and finance a lot lately. You know, it's funny how some people can go from being great golf buddies to, well, not so much, just because you don't make them your financial adviser. I guess that's what happens when 'friendliness' isn't enough to guarantee good financial advice.

According to a recent article on MarketWatch, a man had a falling out with his golf buddy after he didn't make him his financial adviser. The article highlights that 'friendliness alone is not a sufficient reason to trust someone with your finances.' It's a good reminder to do our research and not just trust someone because they're a nice person. I mean, who hasn't been tempted to hand over their financial decisions to someone who seems trustworthy, only to find out they might not have our best interests at heart?

In other news, it seems like there's a new kid on the block in the semiconductor space. The Invesco Semiconductors ETF, or PSI, has been making some serious moves. A $10,000 position in this ETF at the end of 2025 was worth around $20,496 by May 26, 2026. That's kind of performance that'll get you invited to some pretty exclusive dinner parties, or at least make your brother-in-law take notice. And it's not just individual investors who are taking notice - it seems like some of Wall Street's biggest names are getting lapped by this little-known ETF.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.

sources.title: My golf buddy dropped me when I didn’t ask him to be my financial adviser. Was I naive?
